title: Realm
objectives: Explain Realm's live objects and reactivity

sections.markdown: Realm is an object-oriented mobile database from MongoDB with **live objects** — query results stay in sync with the DB automatically — and optional cloud sync via MongoDB Atlas Device Sync.

rows: Realms are per-isolate; use frozen for cross-isolate reads

sections.markdown: The live-object model is Realm's superpower: when you write to the DB, any object references you hold reflect the change immediately. Combined with Device Sync, this makes real-time collaborative mobile apps unusually simple.
Passing live objects across isolates. Live objects are tied to their Realm's isolate — snapshot them (`freeze()` in some SDKs) or re-query on the target isolate.
For offline-first with server sync, Atlas Device Sync + conflict rules can replace a lot of custom infrastructure. Just watch the cost model for large user bases.

flashcards.front: What are live objects in Realm?
flashcards.back: Query results that auto-update with the DB — reads always see the latest committed state.
MongoDB's managed sync that keeps Realm data in sync across devices with conflict resolution.
Realms and live objects are per-isolate; don't pass live objects between isolates.

quiz.explain: Live objects reflect DB changes without re-querying.
Live objects are tied to the Realm's isolate.

challenges.text: Define a model and CRUD via a local Realm.
Use RQL to filter and observe changes reactively.
Model a one-to-many relation and query across it.
Prototype Atlas Device Sync with two devices and a conflict scenario.

interviewQuestions: How does Atlas Device Sync work?
How do you handle conflict resolution?
